title: Atelier MCP HTTP/SSE egress audit record
description: One record per outbound HTTP request to a remote MCP server (transport: http|sse). Spec §12 line 656: 'MCP HTTP/SSE servers count as egress targets and are logged the same way as LLM providers — `provider` field on the audit record carries the MCP server name.' Lives in the same on-disk `audit.log` as `subprocess_egress.v1.json` and `egress.v1.json`, discriminated by `kind`. Authorization-bearing headers are never written: the launcher strips them before serialization (see `crates/atelier-core/src/mcp/http_launcher.rs`).

kind.description: Discriminator. Distinct from `subprocess-egress` and `model-call` rows sharing the same `audit.log`.

timestamp.description: RFC 3339 timestamp at the moment the harness initiated the request.

provider.description: Name of the MCP server (`manifest.name`). Matches the `name` regex from `mcp_servers.v1.json` so a row is traceable to a single registration.

url.description: Full endpoint URL the request was directed at. Query strings + fragments are kept (they're routing data — secrets-in-URL is an anti-pattern the redaction layer flags separately).

phase.description: Which step of the launcher lifecycle produced this request. `handshake` is the initial SSE endpoint negotiation; `list-tools` is the post-handshake probe; `call-tool` is any subsequent tool invocation; `shutdown` is the cancellation roundtrip if the server requires it.

outcome.description: Outcome from the harness's perspective. `success` = request completed with a 2xx response (or, for SSE, the stream stayed open); `failure` = transport / status error; `blocked` = the launcher refused to dispatch (today: `allow_net=false` on an http/sse manifest).

reason.description: Free-form diagnostic note. For `blocked`, names the policy (`allow_net=false`). For `failure`, a short error category (`http-status`, `sse-stream`, `transport-io`). Omitted for `success`.

tool_name.description: When `phase == call-tool`, the name of the invoked tool. Omitted otherwise.
